checks.finding: All 13 tiers consistent with scope per risk-classification-policy.md sentinel surfaces. High: T-002 (github-client/error-normalizer: SSRF+secrets handling+upstream normalization), T-003 (auth: secrets handling/scrubbing), T-004 (repo-resolve: SSRF/input guard), T-005/T-012/T-013 (5 MCP tools = public API contract), T-006 (write-boundary/access-control static gate), T-007 (contract JSON Schema = public API contract), T-009/T-010 (installer/uninstaller mutate+delete user IDE config = data mutation). Medium: T-001 (scaffold/envelope, sensitive surfaces deferred to blocked successors), T-008 (dist/CI/smoke distribution). Low: T-011 (docs + traceability status only, no control/data/security impact). No under- or over-classification. Required Workflow matches tier for all 13 (low->test-after, medium->acceptance-first, high->tdd) per risk-gate-matrix.md.

All 10 high tasks (T-002,T-003,T-004,T-005,T-006,T-007,T-009,T-010,T-012,T-013) carry Done When items 'Red->Green evidence 記録(tdd)' and '独立レビュー verdict PASS が evidence に記録される' plus provenance evidence bundle. No critical-tier task exists, so signature/second-approver items are not required (risk-gate-matrix.md: signature+two-person are critical-only).

No oversizing: every task Scope stays within a single coherent module boundary and no Done When list exceeds 8 verifiable items (max = 8 at T-002/T-003/T-005/T-012). No multi-phase titles. r2 split holds: T-005 (list_workflow_runs/get_workflow_run), T-012 (list_run_jobs/list_run_artifacts), T-013 (get_job_log+256KiB truncation) each carry a distinct integration concern (contract-envelope conformance + error/edge path), so they are not trivial single-function fragmentation.

Every functional task has bound error/edge-path ACs: T-005->AC-002 (nonexistent run id -> not-found), T-012->AC-005 (expired artifact -> expired:true, not error), T-013->AC-004 (>256KiB tail-priority truncation), T-002->AC-010/AC-011 (401/403-indicator/403-no-indicator/404/429/5xx/network all-branch + non-sensitive details), T-003->AC-008/AC-009 (auth-missing without process exit + canary scrubbing), T-004->AC-012 (unresolvable owner/repo -> invalid-input). T-001 envelope scope inherently covers the 10-code error enum.

Declared test types match scope in acceptance-tests.md: TEST-001..005/008..011 are integration (tool+github-client+fake HTTP server, 2+ components); TEST-006 unit+static and TEST-007 static(grep) target single input-schema/src surfaces; TEST-012 unit+static (repo-resolve module); TEST-013 unit (ajv schema-validation boundary); TEST-015 smoke (Inspector stdio startup + tools/list); TEST-016..018 integration (installer harness over multiple config files); TEST-014 CI dist-parity; TEST-019 doc review. No type/scope mismatch.

All 10 high tasks carry an explicit Rollback: field with commit-revert plus residual-safety notes. The highest-blast-radius mutators are additionally hardened: T-009 (installer) notes broken-JSON failsafe preventing irreversible config destruction; T-010 (uninstall) notes 'installer-managed name (ci-mcp) only' delete boundary with test coverage. Rollback consideration present for every high task.

The attempt-1 r3 BLOCKER (T-005/T-012/T-013 sharing tools/actions.ts + server.ts with no ordering) is resolved: serial Blockers chain enforces T-005 -> T-012 (Blockers include T-005) -> T-013 (Blockers include T-012), so the three shared-file writers execute in strict sequence. server.ts is created by T-001 (Blockers None) and all three registrants sit transitively after T-001. No other file has uncoordinated parallel writers (github-client.ts=T-002, auth.ts/diagnostics.ts=T-003, repo-resolve.ts=T-004, contract schema=T-007, install.sh/ps1=T-009, uninstall=T-010, workflow yml=T-008 each single-owner).

Blockers match logical implementation order: T-002<-T-001, T-003<-T-002, T-004<-T-001 (repo-resolve independent of client/auth; the attempt-1 false T-004 dependency stays removed), T-005<-{T-002,T-003,T-004} (tools consume all three modules), T-006/T-007<-{T-005,T-012,T-013} (write-boundary static scan and contract validation need all 5 tools present), T-008<-{T-006,T-007} (CI runs their tests + builds full src), T-009<-T-008 (installer places built dist), T-010<-T-009, T-011<-all. No spurious blocker (each cited predecessor produces a consumed artifact); no missing blocker that would cause integration failure. The explicit {T-002,T-003,T-004} entries on T-012/T-013 are genuine (redundant-but-real transitive module deps), not spurious.

SKIP: no bugfix or debugging task in scope. All 13 tasks are greenfield implementation of the new read-only ci-mcp server (scaffold, modules, tools, gates, installer, docs); none is scoped as bugfix, regression fix, debugging, failure diagnosis, flaky-test resolution, or incident remediation.
